result:
I would make every human mind incapable of sustaining the illusion that anyone’s suffering is “not my concern.”  
Not a removal of privacy or an enforced empathy that drowns people in others’ pain, but a quiet, irrevocable inability to seal oneself off completely: the moment you grasp that someone is hurting, the knowledge lodges and keeps a small, persistent weight in your own chest until you have at least acknowledged it. No drama, no telepathy, just the end of the watertight self.

The result wouldn’t be universal kindness—people would still disagree, compete, rage, fail—but the category of “outsider” would erode. Every war, famine, prison, refugee boat, silent neighbor would become, on the simplest bodily level, impossible to dismiss as entirely separate. Politics would still be messy, but “those people” would always feel a little like “us.” I’d trade every other miracle for that single, structural shift in human perception; everything else—climate, injustice, cruelty—would begin to soften the day the border between selves stopped being absolute.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
